pattern: Turning a modular count condition into a running prefix-count-mod-frequency lookup finds every qualifying subarray in a single linear pass.
hints: Replace each element with a 0/1 flag for whether it satisfies nums[i] % modulo == k, then a subarray is interesting exactly when the sum of its flags, mod modulo, equals k.
Track a running prefix sum of flags mod modulo as you scan; for a subarray ending at the current position, you need an earlier prefix value equal to (current prefix - k) mod modulo.
Keep a running count of how many times each prefix-mod value has appeared so far, and at each position add the count for the needed earlier value before recording the current one.

edge_inputs.description: modulo one forces every subarray to qualify
no element ever matches k, but a zero count still equals k
every element matches the target remainder
k at its maximum relative to modulo

stress.description: moderate arrays with a small modulo to create many matching runs
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    modulo = rng.randint(1, 1000)
    k = rng.randint(0, modulo - 1)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums, modulo, k]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def countInterestingSubarrays(self, nums, modulo, k):
        from collections import defaultdict
        cnt = defaultdict(int)
        cnt[0] = 1
        prefix_mod = 0
        total = 0
        for x in nums:
            b = 1 if x % modulo == k else 0
            prefix_mod = (prefix_mod + b) % modulo
            need = (prefix_mod - k) % modulo
            total += cnt[need]
            cnt[prefix_mod] += 1
        return total
